def: s.f.m. (?), A thing that intervenes between two things; a thing that makes a separation between two things or classes of things; interval, bar, partition; connecting link; the interval between death and the resurrection; — a picture of the imagination; a whimsical idea, fashion, or mode; a strange or quaint appearance (syn. ćeshṭā).

def: s.f. Perfume, fragrance, scent, smell, odour; offensive smell, stench; trace, sign, particle, soupçon; (syn. bo, bū)
more: Poet, n. m. P. bāsh ; A. sakū'nat , S. वस् to dwell. Abode; dwelling; residence; lodging. 1. Lauṅḍī kī khush-āmad se susrāl meṅ bās? Prov. Will the favor of the slave-girl ensure you a place in your father-in-law's house? 2. Kām kare talwār kā, phir pānī meṅ bās . Riddle. Ans. Kumhār kā ḍorā the potter's thread. In water lives, but as a sword it cuts. 3. Khaṛī karūṅ ardās, merā hai Gauṛ Bangālā bāsā, Hanumān, Anjnī ke bālak, dījo mujhe dilāsā! Play. Chaubolā . A suppliant here I stand, in Gauṛ Bangāla is my home, O son of Anjnī, Hanumān, may courage from thee come! 4. Har dās ko dās hūṅ, man meṅ dharūṅ yeh ās, Charan kaṅval meṅ dījyo, Pirbhū jī, mohe bās. Bhajan . Hymn. A servant of thy servant, Har, my cherished hope is this— That on your lotus feet, O Lord, I may abide in bliss. bās karnā, basnā , v. n. To dwell; reside; put up; abide.

n. f. S. वास् to scent. 1. Smell; scent; odour; perfume. 1. Bāsī phūloṅ meṅ bās nahīṅ, Pardesī balamwā kī ās nahīṅ . E. Song. When flowers grow stale their scent is wholly gone, Hope of an absent husband's hopeless grown. 2. Paisā nahīṅ pās, to kaise sūṅgheṅ bās . Prov. Not a penny have I got, not the smell of a meal in my lot. (The penniless are turned away from the cook-shop). 2. Fragrance; bouquet; aroma ( su-gand ). 3. Stench; stink; fetor ( saṛāṅd ). Kaisi bās māre hai . Hin. Wom. 4. Mark; sign; token; trace ( bū ). Bhal-mansāt kī bās nahīṅ . 5. Suspicion; inkling; idea; glimpse. Phr. To smell a rat. 6. Whit; jot; particle; bit.
1. s. m. Abode, residence. 2. s. f. Smell, scent, odour.
subs: s.m. A vendor of perfumes, perfumer
s.f. lit. 'Possessing perfume'; a fragrant kind of rice, and of millet.

ety: old P. vaś; Zend vaśaṅh; S. vaśas
def: adj. Enough, sufficient, plenty; very much, too much, a great many; very; — adv. And so; in short, in a word; — intj. Enough! that will do! hold! stay!
more: n. m. S. वश power. See اختيار (1, 4—8). Jānā apne bas, ānā parāë bas . Prov. Go at your leisure, come away at another's pleasure. 1. Command; direction; guidance. Dhīṅvar ke bas parī! Prov. A fairy with a black fisherman! 2. Help; remedy ( chārā ); cure. bas chalnā ; E. basā'nā , v. n. To be able to do, or to avenge oneself; to be capable; to have within one's reach or power. 1. Is maut se chaltā hai bas kiskā, are logo, Joṛī nahīṅ jātī hai ṭūṭī hai agar dhur kī . Wom. Raṅgīṅ. Can any stay the hand of death? Restore once out the living breath? 2. Muṅh se kahe aru nā kare, tā se kahā basāë Tulsī dhoko jo kare, tā pe Rām risāë . Tulsī Dās. Who says and does not, him no power can thrall, Ram's vengeance, Tulsī, on deceit will fall. bas-karan; A. hub . n. m. A charm or spell; philter. Tulsī nīk bachann se sukh upjat chahuṅ or, Basi-karan yehī mantr hai, taj de bachan kaṭhor . Dohā. From kind…

intj. P. bāsh stop. 1. Enough! no more! that will do! that's all. Ādhī roṭī bas. Kāyath haiṅ, ki pas? Prov. Half a loaf's enough. I am a Kāyath, not a beast. 2. Stop! Stay! hold hard! avast! woh! 3. In short; in a word; to sum up; bas bas , Emph. of bas . (Slang). Shut up. bas kar'nā , v. n. 1. To stop; lay or put aside; give up; give over; leave off; hold; pause. 2. To put an end to; finish; close; cut the matter short. Bas kar, mīyāṅ, bas kar, dekhā terā lashkar . Prov. Enough, good Sir, shut up, your grandness we have seen. 3. To be satisfied, satiated, content.
adj. Enough, sufficient, plenty, abundance, too much, very much. بس کرنا bas karnā, To stop, to seize, to put an end to, to have done. بس که bas ḳi, Although.
s. m. Power, will, command, advantage, authority. بس کرنا bas karnā, To overpower, to bring to submission. بس آنا bas ānā, To be obtained, to come into oneʼs power.
subs: intj. (emphat. and colloq.), Cease! have done! no more!
v.n. To stop, have done, cease, desist, to give up, lay or put aside, put an end to, close, finish; to seize; to be satisfied
adv. To such an extent that; — inasmuch as, whereas.

def: s.m. Spring, the vernal season (comprising Ćait and Baisākh, or from the middle of March to that of May); the vernal equinox; — name of one of the rāgs or musical modes of the Hindūs; — small-pox; — garland or wreath of yellow flowers
more: n. m. S. वसन्त. 1. The season of spring from the middle of March to that of May; the vernal equinox. 1. Kuchh basant kī bhī khabar hai? Prov. Know you the spring has come? (Said to one who does not rejoice when it is spring, and ironically to one who laughs when he should weep). 2. Māth par moṭṛī, basant ke gīt! E. Prov. A bundle on his head, he sings of spring! 3. Kuārī ko sadā basant . Prov. Maidenhood's perennial spring! 4. Wahāṅ us ke ghar 1 basant hai, yahäṅ mere ghar 2 bas ant! Rejoicing there and weeping here! 2. One of the rāgs or musical modes of the Hindūs. 3. A garland, wreath, etc. made of yellow flowers. Nahīṅ ghar kanth, le-ke basant āī ghar mālan, Maiṅ kaise pūjūṅ, sakhī, nahīṅ ghar sājan . Song. Bārā-māsā. My husband not by me, the gardener with spring-flowers hath come, How can I offering make, dear friend, my husband not at home. basant phūl'nā, yā khilnā , v. n. To put…

s. f. 1. The spring. 2. One of the Rāgs or musical modes of the Hindūs. 3. The name of the first of the six seasons, comprising Chait and Baisākh. 4. The small pox (a phrase peculiar to the Bangālīs). بسنت پهولنا basant phūlnā, v. n. To put forth the blossoms of mustard plants. آنکهون مين بسنت پهولني ānkhoṅ meṅ basant phūlnī, To dazzle. بسنت کي خبر basant kī ḵẖabar, Knowledge of, or care about, futurity.
subs: v.n. To put forth yellow blossoms (like those of the mustard plant); to have yellow pustules, &c. on the face; to turn yellow or pale (generally with the words muṅh-par; e.g. uske munh-par basant phūlā); yellow sparks to fly (out of the eyes; e.g. āṅkhoṅ-meṅ basant phūlā; hence), to be dazzled, be astonished or amazed
s.f. The vernal festival of the Hindūs on the fifth day of the light half of the month Māgh
basant-rut, s.m. The season of spring
s.f. Knowledge of, or concern about, futurity.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit वसन्त (vasantá). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi بسنت (bsnt /⁠basant⁠/). Doublet of بَہار (bahār) and وَسَنْت (vasant).

def: adj. Of yellow colour, yellow (the colour of spring blossoms, such as those of the mustard-plant); — s.f. Yellow garment; — name of a goddess of small-pox.
more: adj. Yellow (spring). It is so called from the yellow flowers of sarsoṅ (the mustard plant). basan'tī , n. f. 1. A yellow garment. It is worn by the Rājpūts when about to sacrifice themselves in a hopeless conflict. 2. One of the goddesses of small pox.
adj. Of yellow colour. (This was Krishnaʼs favourite colour).

def: s.m. A day; see vāsar.
adv. To a head; to the fore; to an end, at an end
more: To an end. بسرآنا ba-sar ānā, v. n. 1. To come to an end, to succeed, to get away from. 2. (برابري کرنا) To contend or vie with (?). بسرجانا ba-sar jānā, v. n. To be finished, to pass away. بسرکرنا ba-sar karnā, v. a. To bring to an end, to finish, accomplish, execute, spend, pass. بسرليجانا ba-sar lejānā, v. a. To bring to an end, to finish, accomplish, execute, acquit oneʼs self of, outstrip. بسرهونا ba-sar honā, v. n. To be finished, ended, spent, or passed.
subs: v.n. To come to an end, be finished, or accomplished; to succeed; to get away from; to come to the fore, come forward, contend or vie (with)
v.n. To reach the end, to be finished; to pass away
v.t. To bring to an end, finish, accomplish, execute; to spend, pass (time, &c.)
v.t. To bring to an end, &c. ( = ba-sar karnā); to acquit oneself of; to surpass, outstrip
v.n. To come to an end, be finished, spent, or passed; to vie (with), rival.

def: s.m. = baṣārat, q.v.; the eye ( = bāṣira, q.v.). s.f. corr. of A. bashārat, q.v. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'
more: s. m. Seeing, discerning, vision, sight, the eye, understanding, knowledge.

def: v. n. P sar end. 1. To bring to an end; finish; accomplish. 2. To perform; execute; carry into effect. 3. To pass or spend (one's days). Buā, jīnā kyā hai, din ba-sar kartī hūṅ . Mah. Wom. ba-sar ho'nā , v. n. To be passed, finished.

def: s.f. Seeing, perceiving, discerning; sense of sight, vision; insight, understanding, knowledge (cf. baṣīrat).

more: n. m. S. वस् to dwell. A night's lodging; bird's roost. Āj baservā nīar, kāl baservā dūr . Prov. Your resting place quite near to-day, to-morrow far away. baserā lenā, yā karnā , v. n. part. act. (from باشيدن To remain) Inhabitant. (Pers. plur. باشندگان).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian باشنده (bāšanda).

def: s.m. Man, human being; mankind, mortals.
more: s. m. Man, mankind, a human being, mortals.

def: s.m. Outer skin or cuticle; physiognomy, countenance, face, appearance; descriptive roll.
more: n. m. بَشَرَهَُ The external skin. A descriptive roll; the physiognomy; countenance; forehead; look; aspect; appearance; shape; face.

def: s.m. = P بشارت bashārat, bishārat, s.f. (for A. بشارة), Good news, glad tidings, joyful annunciation made to one in sleep or in a vision by some saint; happy dream or vision; divine inspiration, revelation.
more: s. m. See بشارت bashārat. ¶ bashāra (dakh.) A dream, vision ( Harris ). بشاره ديکهنا bashāra dekhnā (dakh.) To dream.

def: s.f. = P بشاره bashāra, bishāra, s.m. (for A. بشارة), Good news, glad tidings, joyful annunciation made to one in sleep or in a vision by some saint; happy dream or vision; divine inspiration, revelation.
more: s. f. Glad tidings, good news.

def: adj. Relating to man, human.
s.f. Humanity, human nature ( = insanīyat, q.v.). s.f. Human nature, humanity; human kindness, affability, politeness, urbanity. (See ādmīyat.)
adv. On condition, conditionally
subs: On the condition that, on the agreement or stipulation that, provided, so that.

def: On condition that, provided that.
s.f. Liveliness, cheerfulness, alacrity.
adv. Extremely, excessively; violently, forcibly.
fem. of bāsha, q.v.
more: s. f. The female of باشه or باشا a kind of hawk or falcon.

def: s.m. Messenger of glad tidings (bashārat).
more: s. m. A messenger of good tidings.
wety: From Arabic بَشِير (bašīr).

def: adj. Brisk, lively, cheerful, sprightly, in good spirits, pleased, delighted (syn. ḵẖẉush).
more: (from بشاشت) adj. superl. In good spirits, pleased, delighted, cheerful.

def: adj. With the inclusion (of, -ke), inclusive (of), including.
adj. Smelling, perfumed, fusty; stale; belonging to the day before
more: n. m. An inhabitant; a dweller, as Brij-bāsī .

adj. 1. Stale; belonging to the day before. 1. Bāsī bache na kuttā khāë . Prov. When there is nothing left of the meal, there is nothing for the dog to take away. 2. Ghar kī puṭkī, bāsī sāg . Prov. A pinch of gram-meal with some stale potherb picked up somehow. (Said to one who brags of the great feats he has performed). 2. Faded; decayed; worn out; passé. bāsī tibā'sī, bāsī tivāsī , n. f. Three days' stale food. bāsī īd , n. f. The day after the fair. bāsī muṅh , n. m. An unwashed face or mouth; an empty stomach. bāsī honā , v. n. To become stale, faded, etc.
adj. 1. Stale. 2. Perfumed. باسي کرنا bāsī karnā, To make stale, to vomit.
adj. mas. (used substantively) Inhabitant; as, वनवासी vana-vāsī, Inhabitant of the woods.
subs: s.f. The day after the fair
v.t. To make stale; to vomit
s.m. Unwashed face or mouth; empty stomach; — adv. With unwashed mouth; fasting
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi باسی (basy) (c. 1672), borrowed from Sanskrit वासिन् (vāsin).

def: adj. & s.m. Dwelling, inhabiting; — dweller, inhabitant (chiefly used as last member of compounds, e.g. ban-bāsī or vana-vāsī, 'inhabitant of the woods'; braj-bāsī, 'inhabitant of Braj or Brij)
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi باسی (basy) (c. 1672), borrowed from Sanskrit वासिन् (vāsin).

def: adv. In the form or mode (of); in the degree.
part. adj. Peopled, inhabited.
adj. Seeing; endowed with mental perception, knowing; (met.) weak-sighted, blind.
more: part. act. (from بصر) Seeing. Met. Blind.

def: s.m. Sense or faculty of seeing; sight.
more: part. act. (from بصر used substantively) Sight, the sense of seeing.

def: s.f. Sight, insight, mental perception; perceptive faculty of the mind; knowledge, understanding, intelligence, discernment; skill; circumspection, prudence.
more: n. f. The faculty of perception; perception; circumspection; discernment; insight; observation.
(from بصر) s. f. Sight, insight, circumspection, prudence.

def: s.m. Head man of a village (prop. basīṭh, q.v.).
adj. Simple, uncompounded, elementary; — s.m. Expanse, surface, superficies (syn. sat̤ḥ).

s.m. Chief person, or head manager, in a village (syn. muqaddam); one who is carefully observant of injunctions, or of all commanded or established rites and usages; (hence) agent, ambassador, envoy, messenger; go-between, intermeddler, busy-body, tale-bearer, mischief-maker; customer (in the language of harlots); — yoke attached to a plough (syn. jūhaṛ).

n. m. H. sāṭhnā to join (?). 1. The yoke attached to a plough. ( jūhaṛ ). 2. An agent; a messenger; envoy ( elchī ). 3. The head manager in a village. (?) 4. A meddler; tale-bearer; mischief-maker; make-bate. Sājan sājan mil gaë, jhūṭe paṛe basīṭh . Prov. The lovers reconciled grown, the tale-bearer's a liar shown. 5. A customer (used by harlots).

s.m. Chief person, or head manager, in a village (syn. muqaddam); one who is carefully observant of injunctions, or of all commanded or established rites and usages; (hence) agent, ambassador, envoy, messenger; go-between, intermeddler, busy-body, tale-bearer, mischief-maker; customer (in the language of harlots); — yoke attached to a plough (syn. jūhaṛ).
s.f. Business of a basīṭh; agency, ambassadorship, &c.
s.m. Name of a tribe of ahīrs or cowherds.
s.m. = basma, q.v.
more: n. m. Cor. of A. vasmā , Hair-dye; a collyrium or tincture for dyeing.
(Arab. وسمه) s. m. Staining (particularly, the beard: or women staining the legs, so as to have the appearance of being dressed in tight drawers, when they are in fact naked).

def: s.m. Painting, staining, dyeing; dye, collyrium, tincture (see wasma).
see under وسمه wasma.
s.f. See s.v. bās, 'perfume.' s.f. Perfume, fragrance, scent, smell, odour; offensive smell, stench; trace, sign, particle, soupçon; (syn. bo, bū)
more: n. m. H. bās perfume. A fragrant kind of rice and of millet.
subs: s.m. A vendor of perfumes, perfumer
s.f. lit. 'Possessing perfume'; a fragrant kind of rice, and of millet.

ety: bās˚ = Prk. वासे(इ) or वास(इ) = S. वासय(ति), rt. वास्
def: v.t. To perfume, scent.
s.f. Inclination, desire, expectation, trust, confidence; fancy, imagination.
s.m. Covering, cloth, wrapper, &c. ( = basan, q.v.; — syn. beṭhan; lapeṭan); bank, banking-firm.
more: v. n. S. वसन from वस् to dwell. 1. To dwell; abide; occupy; settle. 1. Man meṅ base, so supne dase . Prov. What fills the mind night-dreams will find. 2. Jal meṅ base kamodnī, chandā base akās, Jo jan jāke man base, so jan tāke pās . Dohā. The lily in the water lives, the moon dwells in the sky, The life that in the mind doth dwell's the life that's ever nigh. 3. Chor ke jī meṅ chorī base . Prov. Theft dwells in the thief's mind. 4. Pītam, pīt lagāë-ke dūr des jin jāo, Baso hamārī nāgrī, ham māṅgeṅ, tum khāo . Dohā. My heart taken from me, love, to distant lands you must not speed, Live with me in my village here, I'll beg and you shall feed. 5. Harnā pās base kastūrī, bhed nahīṅ pāyā. Bhajan . The musk that's in the deer he vainly seeks both far and near. 2. To camp; encamp; pitch one's tent. 3. To lodge; perch; roost; nestle. 4. To thrive; prosper; flourish. Jhūṭoṅ ghar nahīṅ bastā . T…
n. m. 1. A wrapper; a banker's cloth on which he puts his scales and weights. 2. A bank; a banking firm. Dillī meṅ do purāne basne haiṅ .
v. n. H. bās smell. To smell; to be scented or perfumed. Gulāb meṅ basīṅ haiṅ gaṅḍeryāṅ poṅḍe kī . Delhī streetcry. Rose-scented sugarcane, come buy.
v. n. To dwell, inhabit, settle, reside, to abide, to be peopled.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀲𑀇 (vasaï), from Sanskrit वस॑ति (vásati), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hwásati, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂wéseti, from *h₂wes- + *-eti.

ety: bas = Prk. वस(इ) = S. वस(ति), rt. वस्
def: v.n. To dwell, abide; to be peopled, be settled, be populated, be cultivated; to be full, be well-peopled; to settle, encamp, lodge, perch, roost; to prosper, flourish, thrive.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀲𑀇 (vasaï), from Sanskrit वस॑ति (vásati), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hwásati, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂wéseti, from *h₂wes- + *-eti.

def: v.n. To be perfumed, be scented, to smell.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀲𑀇 (vasaï), from Sanskrit वस॑ति (vásati), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *Hwásati, from Proto-Indo-European *h₂wéseti, from *h₂wes- + *-eti.

def: s.m.f. (?), Perfume, odour, &c. = bās (syn. su-gandh).
more: W; v. a. To scent; perfume.

n. f. Hin. 1. Smell; scent; odour; perfume. Deotā bāsnā ke bhūke haiṅ . Prov. Gods hunger after the smell only (of the offerings which are the daily meal of the worshipper when made in the house, and the perquisite of the priest when made in the temple). 2. Desire; wish ( ichchhȧ ). Tan sukhāë pinjar kare, dhare rain din dhyān, Tulsī miṭe na bāsnā, binā bichāre gyān . Tulsī Dās.
s. f. Desire, inclination, choice. ¶ bāsnā ( वासन r. वास Perfume) v. a. To scent, to perfume.

def: s.f. Purse (syn. khuttī, thailī).
more: n. f. H. bās dwelling. A purse ( thailī ).

def: s.m. Abode, residence, &c. = bas-bās, q.v. s.m. Abode, dwelling, residence ( = basobās).
more: s. m. Home, dwellingplace, residence, country.

def: s.m. A caste of men whose occupation it is to make baskets of split bamboos; a member of that caste, a basket-maker; a member of the sweeper caste in a village community.
(for A. بصرة), prop. n. The city commonly called Bassorah, on the Persian Gulf.
s.m. Spreading or extending out, distension, diffusiveness.
more: s. m. Diffusiveness, spreading or extending out.

def: part. Bound; shut; frozen
more: n. f. S. वस्तु from वस् to dwell. See اسباب bast-biddyā , n. f. A. ilm-i-tabaī , S. पदार्थविद्या Physics. bast-bal; A. P. tabaī zor . ( Physics .) Physical agents or agency. bast ke gun, yā subhāo . ( Physics .) General properties of matter. bast-gur, yā nem; qāëdah-i-ilm-i-tabaī . Physics; Physical laws. bur'ī bast , Hin; harām , Mah. n. f. lit . Filth. 1. Dirt; filth; excretion; sewage. Mujhe tum se ek kauṛī zyāhdah lenī burī bast hai . 2. An unlawful, forbidden, or unclean thing. Tere hāth kī chīz mujhe burī bast hai .
s. f. Thing, matter, substance, chattels, goods, baggage.
subs: v.t. To bind or collect together
v.n. To be bound or collected.

def: s.m. = بسته basta, q.v.
more: s. m. The cloth in which a bundle is tied up, wrapper, a bundle.

ety: Pehl. baśtak; Zend basta, rt. band; S. baddha, rt. bandh
def: part. adj. & s.m. Bound, shut, closed, fastened, folded up; frozen, congealed; — cloth in which anything is folded up, wrapper; parcel, bundle (as of papers or books), bale
more: n. m. بستن to bind. A bundle (of papers or books); a wrapper; a satchel ( juz-dān ). bastah islāh denā, yā bāṅdhnā , v. a. To tie up a bundle or shut up a pen-tray, used chiefly by Kāyasths as a mark of respect to the implements of their profession; to close an office.
part. pass. (from بستن) Bound, shut, closed, fastened; frozen. بسته کار basta-kār, adj. Distressed. (Plur. بستگان). s. m. Cloth in which any thing is folded up, a parcel, a bundle: see بستا.
subs: basta bāṅdhnā, v.n. To tie up a bundle, fold up and put away (papers, &c. in an office), to close (office).
wety: From Classical Persian بسته (basta, “sealed, closed, shut, package”).

def: adj. Populated, inhabited (a village).
s.f. Binding, contraction, stoppage, obstruction, constipation, concretion, congelation.
more: s. f. Binding, contraction, constipation, concretion, congelation.

def: s.m. Servant, attendant, minister.
s.m. Servant, attendant, minister.
adj. Old, ancient, past; (in Southern India) after, afterwards ( = baʻd az ān).
s.f. Cloth for binding together bundles of papers, books, inkstands, &c.; wrapper.
more: s. f. A cloth for binding together bundles of paper, books, ink-stands, &c.

def: s.m. The shrub Callicarpa lanata, or C. cana, or C. tomentosa (the root of which is employed in cutaneous diseases. It is also one of the trees used for making charcoal).
more: s. m. The name of a plant. (Callicarpa Americana).

def: s.f. Inhabited place, settlement, colony; village, small town; abode, home; population, inhabitants (syn. ābādī).
more: n. f. S. वसति, Pr. vasahī from वस् to dwell. 1. Plantation; settlement; an inhabited place; a colony; a village; small town; population; inhabitants. 2. Light; joy. See ābādī . 1. Inhīṅ ke dam kī bastī hai . 2. Na basne dīhauṅ tuhār purvā, Jo bastī hamrī ujāṛ huīëṅ . E. Song. Ṭhumrī . Not your estate shall prosperous stand, If mine you ruin with strong hand. (Said to the husband who proposes to install a co-wife in another house).
s. f. An abode, a village, a peopled or inhabited place, a population.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi بستی (bsty) (c. 1657), semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit वसति (vasati).

def: s.m. Cloth, clothes, dress, garment, vesture, raiment, apparel.
more: Ped. n. m. S. वस्त्र from वस् to put on. Wearing apparel; clothes; garment; raiment. Chale sursurā, kampe deh; Hai koī dātā bastar de? Mendicant's cry. The cold wind blows, I'm shivering froze, Some generous soul bestow some clothes.
s. m. Cloth, clothes, raiment, apparel.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بِسْتَر (bistar).

def: s.m. Site or foundation of a house, &c. (see vāstu).
s.f. Thing, matter, article, commodity, substance, wealth, property, means; things, goods, chattels, baggage (syn. asbāb)
subs: bast-ke gun, s.m. General properties of matter

n. prop. Name of Kr̤ish̤ṇa, the son of Vasudeva.
s.f. The world, the earth.
more: s. f. The world, the earth.

def: s.m. The chief of serpents, which is fabled to support the universe, and which was used as a string to whirl the mountain Mandar in churning the ocean for the amr̤it, &c.
s.m. The chief of serpents, which is fabled to support the universe, and which was used as a string to whirl the mountain Mandar in churning the ocean for the amr̤it, &c.

def: s.m. A kind of axe used by carpenters, an adze (dialectic forms are bāsilā, basilā, basulā).
more: Gaṛh. n. m. H. sālnā . A kind of axe used by carpenters; an adze.
s. m. A kind of axe used by carpenters, an adz.

def: s.f. A mason's or builder's hatchet, an instrument for cutting bricks or stone.
more: n. f. A small adze used by masons.
s. f. An instrument for cutting bricks.

def: adv. In the appearance (of), in the form or guise (of); in the manner (of), by way (of); in appearance, apparently, outwardly.
more: In the manner, by the way.

def: s.f. = bāṅsrī, q.v. s.f. Pipe, fife, flute, flageolet
subs: v.n. To pipe, to play on the flute.

def: s.f. = beswā, q.v. s.f. Prostitute, harlot, courtesan; crafty or cunning woman.

def: s.f. Speech, language, word, saying, conversation, talk, gossip, report, discourse, news, tale, story, account; thing, affair, matter, business, concern, fact, case, circumstance, occurrence, object, particular, article, proposal, aim, cause, question, subject
more: n. f. S. वार्ता speech, Pr. vattā . 1. Word; vocable ( bol ); speech; saying ( bain ). 1. Bāteṅ iskī misrī ḍalyāṅ, bol chhuārā lāge rī, Lalwā mainū pyārā lāge rī . W. Rus. Wom. Song. Its prattling sweet as lumps of sugar, its tones as dates, I love the little darling so! 2. Bātan hāthī pāë, bātan hāthī pāoṅ . Prov. Words may bring you an elephant, and words may bring you under his feet. (An Asiatic. punishment). 2. Parley; verbal communication; conference; audience. Bāt kī bāt, khurāfāt kī khurāfāt . Prov. Truth in jest. 3. Ṭalk; say; parlance; discourse; speech; oration; chat; chit-chat; conversation; gossip ( guft-gū ). 4. Idle talk; mere talk ( nikammī bāteṅ ). Rahm khā-kar mere ahwāl pe jā rī, annā, Rāt bātoṅ meṅ yahāṅ tū na gaṅvā rī, annā . Wom. Raṅgīṅ. 5. Remark; comment; observation; allegation. Tumhārī bāteṅ likh rakhne ke qābil haiṅ . 6. Inuendo; sneer; taunt ( tٴana ); harsh wo…

Maṛ. n. m. S. वट् to divide. A measure of weight; a weight. Tumhāre bāṭ kam haiṅ. bāṭ tarāzū , n. m. Weights and scales. bāṭ-chhāp , n. m. Stamping weights and measures. bāṭ-chhapāī , n. f. A fee charged for stamping the weights. bāṭ hāṛnā, yā haṛ'nā , v. a. 1. To make a weight; to test the accuracy of a weight. 2. Hin. Wom. To tease; worry; harass. Rāt din mujh se bāṭ hāṛtā hai . Wom.

n. m. S. वाट Path; way; byway; road. bāṭ joh'nā, dekh'nā, yā her'nā, rāh dekhnā , v. a. To look out for one; wait one's coming; expect one. Johat Lālan jī kī bāṭ, dukhai morī aṅkhyā . Song. Birah . My eyes stretched forth Kishan to see, Sore ache with long expectancy. bāṭ chalnā, yā kāṭnā , v. n. To travel along a road; to travel; journey. Akelā chalye na bāṭ, jhāṛ baiṭhye khāṭ . Prov. Alone a journey never take, nor sit before your bed you shake. bāṭ dikhānā , v. a. 1. To show the road; direct one on his way. 2. To keep one waiting, or in expectation. bāṭ-dikhāū ; P. rah-numā ; A. hādī , n. m. A guide; conductor; runner; pilot. bāṭ roknā , v. n. To block up or close a road; to stop one on the road.

s. f. 1. Speech, language, word, saying, report, discourse. 2. Account, subject, question, cause, business, affair, matter, circumstance, particular, article. بات باندهنا bāt bāndhnā, To sophisticate, to prevaricate. بات بڙهانا bāt baṛhānā, To prolong a contest. بات بگاڙنا bāt bigāṛnā, To mar a plot, to spoil. بات بنانا bāt banānā, To make up a story, to make excuses. بات پانا bāt pānā, To accomplish oneʼs wishes, to effect oneʼs purpose. بات پهيرنا bāt phernā, To equivocate. بات پهينکنا bāt phenknā, To jeer, to mock, to speak at (but not to). بات ٿالنا bāt ṭālnā, To put off, to excuse oneʼs self. بات چلانا bāt chalānā, To converse, to start a subject. بات چيت bāt chīt, s. f. Conversation, chitchat, confabulation, discourse, talk. بات چيت کرني bāt chīt karnī, To converse, confabulate, to discourse, to talk. بات دهرانا bāt dharānā, To persist in making excuses, to put off, to evade. بات ڐ…
s. m. Pain in the joints, rheumatism.

def: s.m. Bamboo-lath, a thin slip of bamboo; — an oblong stone on which condiments, &c. are levigated ( = baṭṭā, q.v.).
more: n. m. H. baṭ a twist. 1. Deficiency; defect; fault; flaw ( khoṭ ) 2. Stain ( dhabbā ); stigma; blot; pollution. Jā ke dharm lago hai baṭṭā; Sadā rahe jī tākār khattā . Biharī Lāl. The man whose piety has e'er a stain, Through all his life no pleasure can obtain. 3. Loss; discount; exchange, Mathrā ke rupaë pe kyā baṭṭā lagtā hai? 4. A cylinder of wood or stone; a small roller. Sil baṭṭā . 5. A jewel box; a betel box ( ḍibbā ). 6. A round looking glass ( shīshā ). Chāl terī ḍagmag, mukh baṭṭā aisā. Rāt rahī sāth, dinā ghūṅgaṭ kaisā! E. Play. Chaubolā . 7. A juggler's inverted cup under which he places or transfers the balls from one cup to another ( piyālā ). baṭtā denā , v. n. 1. To suffer or pay discount. 2. To make up a loss or deficiency. baṭṭā ḍhāl , adj. Level; even ( chauras ); plane. — — n. m. Ruin; complete destruction. baṭṭā lagānā , v. a. 1. To charge discount ( baṭṭā lenā ). 2…
s. m. 1. A bamboo lath, a thin slip of bamboo. 2. An oblong stone on which powders are levigated.

ety: batā˚ = batāw˚ = Prk. वत्तावे(इ) = S. वर्त्त (rt. वृत्)+ आपय(ति)
def: s.m. A bracelet or ring of metal (worn by men and women); — an under-turban (used to swell out and-give shape to the outer turban, or to keep it from being soiled by the perspiration of the head); an old or ragged turban; — livelihood, maintenance, support, help, aid.
more: v. a. S. वद् to speak. 1. To speak; converse; talk. Rus. Chhailā haule batāo, koī sun lego . Wom. Song. 2. To tell; relate; describe ( kahnā ). 3. To name; call; term; designate ( nām lenā ). 4. To explain; tell; teach; instruct ( sikhānā ). Chār harf inheṅ bhī batāo . 5. To make known; account for ( zāhir k ). 6. To disclose; discover; betray ( kholnā ). 7. To indicate; suggest; signify; express. 8. To point out; shew; direct ( dikhānā ). 1. Ut se andhā āë hai, it se andhā jāë. Andhe se andhā milā, kaun batāē rāë! Dohā. There comes a blind man, here another, say, When blind meets blind, which is to shew the way. 2 Chandr-badan, mirg-lochnī, kar kesan kī chhāṅ, "Ham ko ḍagar batāë de ūṅchī kar-ke bāṅ ." Dohā. Bright as the moon her form, fawn-eyed, her charms her tresses shade, "Shew me my way, O fair one, pray, with one round arm upraised." 9. To set one to some work; to employ or engag…

n. m. 1. A metal bracelet, E.; a pattern bangle. W. 2. An under turban which gives rotundity and apparently extra size to the turban which is worn over it; an old ragged turban. 3. Maintenance; support; help; aid. Khān Khānā, jis ke khāne meṅ batānā . Prov. Of Khān Khānā it's told, his dinners did enfold rich gifts of gold.
v. a. S. वट् to divide. 1. To cause to be shared or divided. 2. To draw away or divert (one's attention).
v. a. 1. (perhaps, from वदन Speaking, telling) To point out, to shew, indicate, signify, to explain, to teach, to tell. 2. To appear. 3. s. m. An under turband. 4. A small bracelet of metal.
wety: From Sanskrit वत् (vat) + ـآ (-ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

ety: S. वार्त्त, rt. वृत्, or rt. वत्+H. caus. aff. ānā
def: v.t. To seem to be, to appear
wety: From Sanskrit वत् (vat) + ـآ (-ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

more: n. m. S. वाटिक, H. bāṭ path. 1. A traveller; passenger; wayfarer. Jale parāī dhī, haṅse batāū log . Prov. Another's daughter burns, wayfarers laugh! 2. A stranger; foreigner; alien. 1. Sīkhegā nāū kā, kaṭegā baṭāū kā . Prov. The barber learns by shaving strangers. 2. Chho mat ho nār baṭeū par, Rūkhā sūkhā po-kar rakh de, Uske dhore tauṅ ṭīkar . W. Rus. Song. Wife, be not angry with the stranger guest, Brown bread or white, or coarse or fine, the best Before him set with generous kindness pressed.
subs: s.f. Division of produce or crops
s.m. A cultivator who is a shareholder with the landlord in the crop.

def: s.m. Divisibility.
s.m.? Land in a state fit for the plough.
more: (contract. of بدتر) adj. compar. Very bad, worse.
adj. Mutilated, wanting the tail (an animal); destitute of good qualities. See ابتر.

def: adj. Dock-tailed (animal), wanting the tail; mutilated; — destitute of good qualities ( = abtar, q.v.).
s.m. Name of a tribe of Gūjars, q.v.
adj. Worse; very bad.
adv. Aside, apart ( = bar t̤araf).
adv. By the way; by way (of), by means (of).
adv. In the mode or manner (of); by way (of).
s.f. Wind, air
more: Poet. n. f. S. वात, P. bād . Wind; air ( bāo ). 1. Āṅdhar kukar, batāse bhūke . E. Prov. The blind dog howls at the wind. 2. Āṅdhī ke āge, benā kī batās! E. Prov. Before the wind a fan!
s. f. Wind, air. بتاس پهيني batās-phenī, s. f. A kind of sweetmeat, sugar-cakes.
subs: s.f. A kind of sweetmeat (cf. next).

def: v.n. To dissolve or melt away like a batāsā, to fade rapidly away; to become a shadow.
more: n. m. 1. ( Poet .) A bubble. Pānī kā batāsā . 2. A sweetmeat or a cake of a spongy texture, filled with air. 3. A firework. 1. Chakkī pe chakkī, chakkī meṅ batāshā, Dūlhā kī māṅ kare tamāshā . Boys' Doggerel. Millstone with stone doth grind, and in it flummeries sweet, See how the bridegroom's mother revels in the treat. 2. Batāsā le ( Batā sāle ). Mazāq. Pun. Come buy my patāsas (Speak brother-in-law— an abusive epithet). 3. Sādhū ko kiyā savād, guṛ nahīṅ, batase hī sahī . Prov. The holy man, no taste has he; no guṛ , ah well! batāsā let it be. batāsā sā ghulnā , v. n. To melt away; to become a shadow; to be reduced to mere skin and bone. Ab ṭoṭkā mujhe muī saukan kā khul gayā, Lo chār din meṅ bachchā batāsā sā ghul gayā . Wom. Saīyad. Her wicked charms that good-for-nothing co-wife sure hath wrought, In four days' time worn to a shadow hath my child been brought.
s. m. A kind of sweetmeat, sugar-cakes (of a spongy texture, or filled with air, as the word implies); a bubble.

def: v.n. To dissolve or melt away like a batāsā, to fade rapidly away; to become a shadow.
more: s. m. A kind of sweetmeat, sugar-cakes (of a spongy texture, or filled with air, as the word implies); a bubble.

def: s. m. See بتاشا bat-āshā.
adj. (f. -ī), Indicating, indicatory, representing, &c.
subs: s.f. (Math.) Line of representation or demarcation; — batātī lakīr nāp, s.f. Analytical Geometry (syn. bīj-gaṅit nāp; ḵẖarāsh-ë-maḵẖrūt̤).

ety: S. वर्त, or H. बाट 'road'+S. aff. आकु
def: s.m. Wayfarer, traveller; highway robber (?).
s. m. A traveller, a robber.
adv. After the manner (of), like; in a manner; by way (of)
more: By the way, in the manner.
subs: adv. In a manner peculiar to himself (or themselves); in his (or their) own way; on his (or their) own account; of himself; of themselves.

def: s.f. A flatulent or œdematous swelling; tumour; boil (syn. phuṛiyā, bal-toṛ).
more: E. n. f. H. bāo wind. A flatulent or œdematous swelling.
s. f. A flatulent or œdematous swelling.

def: v.t. = batānā, q.v. s.m. A bracelet or ring of metal (worn by men and women); — an under-turban (used to swell out and-give shape to the outer turban, or to keep it from being soiled by the perspiration of the head); an old or ragged turban; — livelihood, maintenance, support, help, aid.
more: v. a. To extinguish. See بجهانا

def: s.m. The shaddock or pumelo, Citrus decumana (syn. ćakotrā).

v.t. = baṭānā, baṭwānā, q.v. s.m. A bracelet or ring of metal (worn by men and women); — an under-turban (used to swell out and-give shape to the outer turban, or to keep it from being soiled by the perspiration of the head); an old or ragged turban; — livelihood, maintenance, support, help, aid.
(باٿ Road, and گهاٿ Quay, &c.). adv. Somewhere or other.

n. f. pl. of bāt , q. v. bāteṅ baghār'nā, yā maṭhār'nā , v. n. lit . To season one's speech. To talk big, grand or fine; to talk at a great rate. Bas na bāteṅ baghāro, aë nāseh, Tum bhī ākhir to hūr chāhte ho . Saīyad. Cease, moralist, your preaching vain, in any case You know that in your heart you long a Hūr t'embrace. bāteṅ chiknānā , E; chiknī chupṛī bāteṅ karnā , W. v. n. lit . To grease one's words. To have an oily tongue; to flatter; wheedle. Chiknī bāteṅ jan patyāo . E. Prov. Trust not an oily tongue. bāteṅ sunānā , v. a. 1. To revile; abuse, insult. Us ne mujhe saiṅkṛoṅ bateṅ sunāīṅ . 2. To speak harshly; upbraid; reproach. 3. To repeat one's adventures; narrate stories. bāṭeṅ sunnā , v. n. 1. To listen to; pay attention to or heed; give or lend an ear. 2. To bear or brook abuse. bāteṅ lagānā , v. a. 1. To tell tales; speak ill of; backbite; poison one's mind ( kān bharnā ). 2. To misrepresent; slander; calumniate. bāteṅ milānā, hāṅ meṅ hāṅ milānā , v. a. To say ditto; say yes; agree with; echo (an opinion); be in harmony with. idhar udhar kī bāteṅ , n. f. Gossip; idle-talk; town-talk. Idhar udhar kī bāteṅ hone lagīṅ. baknī bāteṅ , n. f. Indecent talk or acts. khāngī bāteṅ , n. f. Private or domestic affairs. ras kī bāteṅ ; Poet. ras-batyāṅ , n. f. lit . Juicy words. Amorous conversation; lovetalk; the sweet music of love. 1. Man chhīn līyā kar ras batyāṅ . Brij. Song. 2. Joban javānī haī din das kī, Mīṭhā bol, bāteṅ kar ras kī . Song. Marahṭī . Beauty and youth in ten days will fleet past, Let mild and loving words your life outlast. kahne kī bāteṅ , n. f. Mere words or brag; talk merely; nonsense. mīṭhī bāteṅ , n. f. Sweet or honeyed words; soft tender words; friendly or seductive speeches.
s.m. Holder of a share, &c. = baṭaniyā.
s.f. The quail, Perdix olivacea.
more: s. f. A kind of quail (Perdix olivacea. Buch. ).

def: n. f. S. वर्त्तक A quail; Perdix olivacea , Buch.
s.m. Way, road; path, pathway, passage (syn. bāṭ and baṭiyā).
s.m. Hut or shed in the jungle (used by graziers); cattle-fold, open place in a field into which cattle are driven at night; pasture-ground (syn. baṭhān).
more: E. n. m. S. अवस्थानं abode. Cattle fold; an open place where cattle are driven in at night ( khaṛak ). Kānī gāë ke alge bathān? E. Prov. Has the blind cow a separate stall? (Must I be put out of the society of the most worthy because I am less worthy).

def: s.m. = bathān, q.v. s.m. Hut or shed in the jungle (used by graziers); cattle-fold, open place in a field into which cattle are driven at night; pasture-ground (syn. baṭhān).
s.f. Heap of dried cow-dung.
more: n. f. A heap of dried cowdung ( māṅd ).

def: v.n. To enter ( = paiṭhnā, baiṭhnā, q.v.). v.n. To enter (into, -meṅ), run or rush (in); to penetrate (into); to force an entry (into); to pervade; to intrude; to infiltrate
subs: (-ke andar), v.n. (intensive) To run or rush (into), &c. = paiṭhnā
v.t. To allow (one) to enter, to give entrance (to), to admit
s.m. One who enters, one who forces himself (in), an intruder; — a diver; a man who cleans out wells.

def: v.n. To be twisted, &c. = baṭnā, q.v. v.t. To share, &c. = bāṅṭnā, q.v. v.t. To divide, apportion, distribute, portion out, parcel out, divide into shares; to dispense, dispose of, assign; to participate, go shares in
subs: v.t. To divide, portion out (for, or in the name of, -par), &c. = bāṅṭnā, q.v.
v.t. To participate, go shares in, &c.

def: v.t. To share, &c. = bāṅṭnā, q.v. v.t. To divide, apportion, distribute, portion out, parcel out, divide into shares; to dispense, dispose of, assign; to participate, go shares in

v.t. To participate, go shares in, &c.

def: s.m. = bathuʼā, q.v.
s.m. The pot-herb Chenopodium album; — orach; the herb 'all-heal'; a herb which springs up with the rabīʻ or spring crops.
more: n. m. S. बास्तूक Wild potherb or greens; Chenopodium album ; a weed which grows with the spring crops. 1. Tum ho kis khet ke bathwe? Prov. Saying. In what field sprung this potherb mean? (Said to a boaster). 2. Methī, sāg, aur chaulāī, bathwe kī adhikāī . Song. Sīṭhnā . 'Mong potherbs fine a mess of bathwā cheap a great excess.
s. m. A kind of greens or potherbs (Chenopodium album).

def: s.f. Wick of a lamp, &c. = battī, q.v. s.f. Wick, candle, light, lamp; match; slip of bamboo, &c.; bundle of bamboos; tent or bougie; stick (of sealing-wax); twisted folds of a turban
more: E, n. f. Bread baked over burning ashes; a roll.
s. f. 1. A candle. 2. The wick of a lamp. 3. A tent or bougie put into a wound to keep it open.
subs: (-meṅ), To put a candle in the candlestick; to raise the wick (of a lamp)
(-ko), To show a light; to apply a match (to); to set alight, to burn.

def: s.f. Cake of bread baked upon cinders.
s.f. Site of a building, place; house, dwelling, habitation, home; garden-house, villa; garden, enclosure, &c. ( = bāṛī, q.v.); the groin; rod, mace.
s.f. Word, speech (cf. bati-yānā).
more: s. f. A word. See بات bāt.
wety: Diminutive of بات (bāt).

def: adv. Somewhere or other ( = bāṭ-ghāṭ).
more: s. f. A habitation, dwelling-house, home, a garden-house.

def: s.f. Villa, &c. = bāṭī and bāṛī, q.q.v. s.f. Wick of a lamp, &c. = battī, q.v. s.f. Wick, candle, light, lamp; match; slip of bamboo, &c.; bundle of bamboos; tent or bougie; stick (of sealing-wax); twisted folds of a turban

ety: act. part. of بطل, 'to be false,' &c.
def: adj. False, untrue, wrong, incorrect; fictitious, spurious, unreal, unfounded, unsound; vain, futile, worthless, useless, unprofitable; devoid of virtue or efficacy, of no effect, ineffectual, null, void, of no force, of no account, naught, going for nothing; annulled, abolished, counteracted
more: adj. A. بطل It was or became false. 1. False; not true; fictitious; unreal; fallacious. 2. Spurious; unsound; worthless. 3. Vain; futile; ineffectual; nugatory; useless ( birthā ). 4. Void; null; abolished. 5. Devoid of virtue; of no effect; of no force; of no account; naught. bātil samajhnā, bātil mutasavvar k . v. n. To treat as invalid; treat as a nullity; to set at naught. bātil kar'nā , v. a. 1. To vitiate; render defective; invalidate. 2. To nullify; make void, annul; falsify ( jhuṭlānā ). 3. To rescind; revoke; draw the pen across ( chheknā ). 4. To reverse; abolish; quash; cancel. 5. To supersede; set aside; overrule. 6. To prove false; prove the contrary; disprove; refute; defeat; upset. 7. To thwart; baffle; frustrate; foil. bātil honā , v. n. To be annulled; cancelled; to become void; to come to nought.
(from بطل) adj. False, vain, absurd, futile, ineffectual, ignorant, abolished. باطل السحر bāt̤ilu-s-siḥr, Annuller of enchantment, what or who frustrates incantation.
subs: s.m. Counteractor or annuller of enchantment; that which or he who frustrates incantation
v.t. To regard, or treat, as false, &c.; to treat as a nullity; to set at naught

v.t. To falsify; to prove to be false; to render ineffectual, vain, useless, &c.; to nullify, vitiate, invalidate, make void; to annul, abolish; to set aside, supersede; to frustrate, counteract, thwart
v.t. To consider false, vain, &c. = bāt̤il samajhnā, q.v.
v.n. To be or prove vain, ineffectual, &c.; to come to naught; to become void; to be nullified, frustrated, &c.; to be annulled or abolished.

def: s.f. Vanity, folly, absurdity.

ety: act. part. of بطن, 'to penetrate or enter into'
def: adj. & s.m. Unapparent, hidden, concealed, covert, inward, inner, interior, internal, intrinsic, esoteric (opp. of z̤āhir); — the internal or intrinsic state, character or circumstances (of a man or a thing); the inward part; the secret thoughts; mind, heart; disposition of the mind; recesses of the mind.
more: n. m. Opp. of zāhir . بطن the belly. The inward part; the inside; mind; heart; disposition. Zāhir meṅ Khudā-tars haiṅ, bātiṅ kā Khudā hāfiz! Prov. In appearance a saint, from his inside Heaven preserve us!
(from بطن) s. m. The inward part, the inside, the internal; the heart, the mind. adj. Internal, hidden, concealed.

def: s.f. A species of egg-plant; a kind of brinjal; green or unripe fruit or vegetable (generally); — adj. Unripe, raw, green; soft, tender, delicate ( = komal).
more: n. m. S. वार्त्ता Green unripe fruit or vegetable in general; the egg plant in contradistinction to the mārū , q. v. Ihāṅ kuhaṛ batiyā kou nāhīṅ, Jo tarjani daīkhat mari jāhīṅ . Rāmāyan. In me you see no pumpkin raw, At slightest touch to melt and thaw.
adj. 1. Raw; green; not ripe. 2. Soft; tender; delicate ( komal ).
n. m. One who works in baṭaī .

n. f. 1. Pathway (Diminutive of bāṭ ). Baṭyā kī rāh be-nirbāh . Rus. Prov. The narrow pathway leads astray. 2. Cocoanut. 3. Met . The breasts. Jab hāth āī uskī aṅgyā kī narm baṭṭī, Eagte hī hāth niklī rag rag kī sārī sardī . Nazīr. 4. A small round stone for weighing, grinding, etc.

s.f. A small weight (bāṭ); a small round stone for weighing, grinding, &c.; — a cocoanut; (met.) the breasts (of a woman).

n. m. Deductive Logic. bāt na karnā , v. n. 1. Not to speak; to be silent; hold one's tongue; say nothing; make no reply. Aṅgrez kāle ādmiyoṅ se bāt nahīṅ karte . The English dont talk to the black race. 2. To be proud or haughty. bāt halkī, yā heṭī honā , v. n. To be lowered in one's esteem; to lose one's credit. ādhī bāt , n. f. lit . But half a word. The slightest word; a slightly harsh word. Mujh se kisī kī ādhī bāt nahīṅ sunī jātī. uljhī-huī bāt , n. f. A tangled or complicated affair; a knotty point; a nice point; a dead lock; a difficult task. āmne sāmne kī bāt , n. f. 1. A tete àtete : a conversation held face to face. 2. A connection by marriage. bārīk bāt , A nice point; fine discrimination. Bārīk bāt kā samajhnā mushkil hai . burī bāt , n. f. Bad words or conduct. baṛī bāt , n. f. 1. An important thing; a great matter. 2. A difficult affair ( mushkil kām ). baṛī bāt huī , intj. Thank God! thank Heaven! baṅdhī bāt n. f. An established rule. pech-kī bāt , n. f. Wily, crafty, or delusive language; gammon. chabā-chabā-kar bāt karnā, chabā-ke bāt k. , v. n. 1. To clip one's words; to mouth; speak indistinctly; hum and haw. Yūṅ na bāteṅ chabā-chabā-ke karo, Mehrbāṅ, bāt hai, nabāt nahīṅ . Nāsikh. Dont chop and chew your words now so, this trick eschew, For sure, my friend, words are not candy lumps to chew. 2. Not to speak out or at once; to bring out what one has to say by little and little; to use vague hints and indirections. 3. To drawl, or hum and haw from pride or affectation; to haw ... haw. Tum ne raqīb ko jo ek pān kal dīyā thā, Kartā hai kyā hī, kāfir, bāteṅ chabā-chabā-kar! Niyāz. Since yesterday a pān you dropped into my rival's paw, The rascal drawls and clips his words—it's nothing but "aw ... haw!" choṭī kī bāt . The pith or essence of a matter. chhoṭī, yā adnā bāt , n. f. A small or easy matter; trifling or frivolous employment. Chhoṭī bāt hai . It is n't worth while. sāmne-kī bāt , n. f. Words spoken, or acts done in one's presence, life-time, or age.
s.m. Wayfarer, traveller ( = baṭāʼū, q.v. — The form baṭoʼiyā also occurs).

W. Rus. and E.; v. a. See اِکهتّک 1, 2, 4. 1. Dillī shahar suhāvnā, barse kanchan nīr, Sab ke kanth baṭor-ke le gayā Ālamgīr . Dohā. Ans. Baiṭhi raho santokh se, man meṅ rākho dhīr, Sāīṅ se mintī karo, ki baureṅ Alamgīr . Dohā. "A city lovely is Delhī, bright rains more pleasure give, But Ālamgīr hath taken our husbands, how are we to live." "Wait patiently all ye and summon fortitude, nor burn, And prayers offer up that Alamgīr may safe return." (The petition of the women of Delhi and Alamgīr's reply. Attack of the Deccan in 1685). 2. Ham, saīyāṅ, kūṛā baṭoreṅ, Tum jhoko bhāṛ, raho ghar meṅ, Saīyāṅ, bhoṅjoṅ bhāṛ, raho ghar meṅ . Rus. Wom. Song. Husband, dry leaves I will bring home, You just parch grain and live at home, Oh stay at home and do not roam!
